patches.find: Magnetic pole deviation >18° from 120°E longitude is unprecedented and predictive.
patches.replace: Magnetic pole deviation exceeds 18° east of the 120°E meridian, confirming the post-1990 exponential trajectory.
patches.rationale: WIN-036.c3 minor: update claim to match dome's actual PROS-006 wording (curmudgeon recommendation).

patches.find: part of a batch of five predictions all registered the same day
patches.replace: part of a batch of five predictions (WIN-035, WIN-036, WIN-037, WIN-038, WIN-039 / W009, W010, W011, W013, W015) all registered on 6 March 2026
patches.rationale: WIN-036.c3 minor: name the specific WINs and dome IDs in the batch reference.

patches.find: being 19.3° from 120°E is pure arithmetic
patches.replace: being 19.298° from 120°E is pure arithmetic (the dome's own confirmation cites 139.298°E, exceeding the registered 18° threshold by 1.298°)
patches.rationale: WIN-036.c3 minor: use 19.298° (not 19.3°) matching the dome's own confirmation value, and add explicit margin.

patches.find: "relabels_standard":false
patches.replace: "relabels_standard":true
patches.rationale: WIN-036.c3 moderate: WIN-007 evidence already says "standard geophysics explains" the acceleration — relabels_standard should be true. Flagged by curmudgeon.
